ontology.foodex2_description: Pig meat products — pork belly, cooked
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC used for cooked pork belly; skewer preparation not separately coded in FoodEx2 hierarchy. Closest parent match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for pork belly skewer dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One skewer of pork belly (五花肉串) typically weighs approximately 40 g and represents a single serving as commonly sold at Singapore hawker stalls and BBQ outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Pork 五花肉 (1 Skewer 一串) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for Pork 五花肉, 1 Skewer 一串 (cr_id G00010517). All per_100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100g) supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 10006 (Pork, fresh, belly, raw) as HPB Foodo does not separately report saturated fat fractions; this value is consistent with the reported total fat of 10.5 g/100g and is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed per HPB Foodo (G00010517) and consistent with Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is not applicable for this food as carbohydrate_g = 0.0 per HPB Foodo (G00010517). Pure protein and fat foods do not elicit a meaningful glycaemic response. The University of Sydney GI Database and the peer-reviewed International Tables of GI Values (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008) confirm that foods with negligible carbohydrate content are classified as GI not applicable. glycemic_index is set to null (not 0) per protocol. glycemic_load_per_serving = 0.0 by definition (GL = GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = not applicable × 0 / 100).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100 g — critically elevated, far exceeding the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit pork belly skewer due to its extreme sodium content of 1050 mg/100 g, primarily from soy sauce marinade; even a single 40 g skewer delivers approximately 420 mg sodium, representing 18–28% of the recommended daily sodium limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and sugar content is favourable for glycaemic control, but high saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g) and cholesterol (89 mg/100 g) elevate insulin resistance risk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pork belly skewer does not raise blood glucose directly due to its negligible carbohydrate content, but individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to manage saturated fat intake and associated insulin resistance risk, and should be aware that marinade sugars may not be fully captured in the reported nutrition data.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combined burden of saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g), cholesterol (89 mg/100 g), and sodium (1050 mg/100 g) poses significant cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of pork belly skewer due to its high saturated fat, cholesterol, and sodium content, all of which contribute to dyslipidaemia, arterial stiffness, and elevated blood pressure; if consumed, portion should be restricted to one skewer on an occasional basis only.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory red meat with high saturated fat and arachidonic acid content, compounded by extreme sodium load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pork belly is a pro-inflammatory red meat rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) that amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways central to RA; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute with lean poultry, fish, or plant-based protein and avoid the high sodium content that worsens RA-related tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork belly skewer (五花肉串, Wǔ huā ròu chuàn) is a popular Chinese-style grilled dish widely available at Singapore hawker centres, BBQ stalls, and supper spots. It consists of thinly sliced or cubed pork belly (五花肉) — the layered cut from the underside of the pig featuring alternating bands of lean meat and fat — threaded onto bamboo or metal skewers and grilled over charcoal or gas flame. The meat is typically marinated in a soy sauce-based mixture that may include garlic, five-spice powder, sugar, and sesame oil, imparting a savoury, slightly sweet, and smoky flavour profile. The texture is tender and juicy with a lightly charred exterior. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides 171 kcal, 19.1 g protein, 10.5 g fat (including approximately 3.8 g saturated fat), and 0 g carbohydrate. Sodium is critically high at 1050 mg/100 g due to the soy sauce marinade, making this dish unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Cholesterol at 89 mg/100 g and saturated fat content also raise cardiovascular concern. The dish is not suitable for halal or kosher diets. Clinically, it is a high-protein, zero-carbohydrate food that does not spike blood glucose, but its high sodium, saturated fat, and cholesterol content require careful portion control for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, Type 2 Diabetes, or rheumatoid arthritis.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 五花肉串?
Can diabetics eat pork belly skewer?
Is pork belly skewer high in sodium?
Is pork belly skewer suitable for hypertension?
How much protein in pork belly skewer?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the canonical HPB Foodo Singapore database entry for Pork 五花肉 (cr_id G00010517) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The HPB Foodo database is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes. Saturated fat value of 3.8 g/100 g is supplemented from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 10006 (cooked pork belly), consistent with the reported total fat of 10.5 g/100 g.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo (G00010517); pork belly in this preparation contains no carbohydrates as the marinade contribution is negligible and reported as 0 g/100 g by HPB. Sugar_g is confirmed zero per the same source. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a pure meat product. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (carbohydrate_g = 0.0); gi_category set to 'not_applicable' and glycemic_index set to null per protocol for low/zero-carbohydrate foods. GI source references the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (Diabetes Care, 2008) peer-reviewed journal as the authoritative basis for classifying negligible-carbohydrate foods as GI not applicable. Saturated_fat_g of 3.8 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked pork belly (USDA ID 10006) as HPB Foodo does not separately report saturated fat; this is consistent with the total fat of 10.5 g/100 g. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed data for these nutrients are not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this specific marinated and grilled skewer preparation, and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 10006 does not provide a complete match for the marinated dish preparation: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractions not reported in HPB Foodo G00010517; USDA SR Legacy 10006 provides partial fatty acid data for raw pork belly but the marinated grilled preparation alters the fatty acid profile and no approved source reports these fractions for the finished dish. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg — full vitamin panel not reported in HPB Foodo G00010517 for this skewer preparation; these fields remain -1 pending dedicated laboratory analysis. phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg — not reported in HPB Foodo G00010517; these mineral fields remain -1 as no approved source provides values for this specific marinated grilled preparation. water_g — moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo G00010517; grilling reduces moisture relative to raw pork belly and no approved source reports moisture for this finished preparation. Serving size of 40 g per skewer is an estimate based on typical Singapore hawker pork skewer portion sizes; no explicit gram weight is provided in the HPB source description '1 Skewer 一串'. FoodEx2 code A00LC is used as a closest_parent match for cooked pork belly; the skewer preparation is not separately coded in the FoodEx2 hierarchy. The dish is not halal as it contains pork.
